# Structured Outputs And Evals

## Summary

Structured outputs and evals make model behavior testable. Use schemas for machine-read data, validate every response, and keep evals small enough to run during ordinary development.

## Structured Output Rules

- Use schemas for model output that will drive code, tools, persistence, routing, or UI state.
- Define schemas in the application language, such as Zod for TypeScript or Pydantic for Python, so validation and documentation share one shape.
- Lower temperature for deterministic extraction, classification, and routing.
- Validate the model response after generation. Do not trust a schema-shaped string until parsing and validation pass.
- On failure, retry with the smallest correction prompt and the same schema.
- Keep free-form prose separate from machine-read JSON.

## Ollama Notes

Ollama supports structured outputs by passing a JSON schema through the `format` field, and documents lower temperature as a reliability tip. The OpenAI-compatible API path can use `response_format`.

Local model behavior can still vary by model family and prompt. Validate the output in Vicode before using it for tool routing, persistence, or automated actions.

## Eval Rules

- Use deterministic fixtures before live-provider checks.
- Test positive, negative, and ambiguous prompts.
- Assert source path, selected title, or schema field values rather than judging prose quality manually.
- Keep eval prompts short and close to real user language.
- Track failures as product evidence: route miss, source miss, schema failure, tool misuse, or verification gap.
- Run live-provider evals only when deterministic tests cannot prove the risk or before provider graduation.

## Useful Eval Cases

- Project Knowledge route selection
- skill auto-selection
- structured task packet parsing
- tool permission classification
- provider compatibility output parsing
- transcript evidence rendering

- Ollama structured outputs: https://docs.ollama.com/capabilities/structured-outputs
- OpenAI retrieval guide: https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/retrieval
